good: Drive the hips up and squeeze the glutes hard at the top
Push the knees out gently against the band the whole set
bad: Arching the lower back to finish the rep

good: Stay in a quarter-squat and keep tension on the band the whole time
bad: Letting the feet snap together between steps

good: Take a slow 3-second count on the way down, then stand normally
Drop the back knee, drive the front elbow to the instep, then rotate open
bad: Rushing through — move slowly and find range

good: Short, controlled steps with the knees pushed out against the band
bad: Standing tall — stay low to keep the glutes loaded

good: Drop the back knee straight down toward the floor
Keep the front shin close to vertical and the weight through the front heel
Control the full 3-second descent — that's where the strength is built
bad: Pushing the front knee way past the toes

good: Lower under control to chest height, body in one straight line
Squeeze the glutes and brace to keep the hips from sagging
bad: Letting the hips drop or pike up
Flaring the elbows out to 90 degrees

good: Hinge the hips back slowly and feel the hamstring of the standing leg load
Keep the pelvis square — hips level, don't let the free side rotate open
bad: Rounding the back to reach lower

good: Drive through the heel and squeeze the glute at the top
Keep the band pushed out so the knee doesn't cave
bad: Letting the hips drop or twist toward the working side

good: Step back and lower under control until both knees hit 90 degrees
Drive through the front heel to stand
bad: Crashing the back knee into the floor
Letting the front knee drift inward

good: Hips high, lower the crown of the head toward the floor between the hands
Swap in a 40-second front plank if pike push-ups are too much today
Stay low and keep constant tension on the band
Hold the top hard for 30 seconds, then pulse from a high hip position
Keep the glutes switched on the whole time
bad: Letting the hips sink during the hold

good: Thighs parallel to the floor, back flat on the wall, breathe through the burn
bad: Sliding the hips up to take the load off the quads
